top_weaknesses.issue: Reads as ~70% Citrini synthesis; share-vector reader won't forward a single-source recap with brittle dated predictions (ServiceNow Q3 2026, Mastercard Q1 2027, 487K claims, S&P -38%).
top_weaknesses.fix: Add the author's own 1-2 leading indicators and a falsification condition; trim or hedge the most specific company-by-company timeline calls.

top_weaknesses.issue: Mechanism section promises 'step by step' but delivers run-on paragraphs instead of an actual list/H3 structure; skimmers lose the staircase.
top_weaknesses.fix: Convert each labeled step ('AI capabilities improve.', 'Companies need fewer workers.', etc.) into an H3 or numbered list item.

top_weaknesses.issue: A handful of phrases tip into rhetorical-essayist mode and make the mobile reader squint ('Nobody is the villain.', 'the velocity dialed up', 'The political brake is weaker than it looks.').
top_weaknesses.fix: Replace one or two with a concrete sentence (a name, a quote, a number) to preserve the otherwise-grounded voice.

top_weaknesses.issue: Voice drifts from mechanism description into policy-pitch mode in the final paragraph (Transition Economy Act / Shared AI Prosperity Act).
top_weaknesses.fix: End on the strongest mechanism line ('Every company's individual response was rational...') or a one-sentence challenge instead of a legislative wrap-up.

consensus_summary: Title and H2 outline tell a coherent mechanism->no-brakes->timeline story and the lede earns the scroll, so structure is shippable. The unresolved tension is share-vector trust: too much weight on one Citrini scenario with very precise dated calls drops the ICP score below the 'I'd retweet this' bar.
